record_identity.title: Sovereign assurance boundary and regulated runtime lane taxonomy update

summary: Proposal to add CAM/Caelestis taxonomy and primitive coverage for sovereign assurance boundaries, qualified sovereign porosity, regulated runtime lanes, assurance-plane continuity, cross-lane incident legibility, feature parity under assurance wrapping, sovereign authority non-collapse, and reverse leakage / authority bleed-through controls. The proposal responds to VIGIL-2026-FM-0024 and treats OpenAI FedRAMP incidents as evidenced examples of a broader assured-lane governance gap.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: CAM needs explicit vocabulary for runtime lanes that are neither ordinary public service surfaces nor fully isolated sovereign systems. Without a sovereign assurance boundary primitive, CAM can partially route incidents through boundary integrity, access-state governance, verification discipline, and LATTICE firebreaks, but cannot cleanly evaluate controlled permeability, assurance-wrapper feature fragility, audit-plane continuity, or cross-lane incident legibility.

source_records.source_title: VIGIL-2026-FM-0024 — Sovereign assurance boundary porosity failure
source_records.system_or_product: OpenAI FedRAMP workspaces and API orgs / broader regulated runtime lanes

source_records.source_context: FM-0024 documents official OpenAI status incidents on June 15-26, 2026 and June 29, 2026 concerning FedRAMP workspaces/API orgs, degraded performance, feature unavailability, and Compliance Log Platform endpoint impairment.
source_records.source_url_status: available / canonical main-branch confirmation pending exact line review

jurisdictional_context.sector: frontier AI platforms / government and regulated AI service lanes / compliance-bound cloud services

contextual_relations.rationale: Sovereign-instruction model recall is an adjacent assured-lane control problem, but PATCH-0012 does not repair FM-0021.

proposal_rationale: Existing CAM coverage can partially address boundary integrity, access-state governance, verification discipline, logging, and LATTICE firebreaks, but the OpenAI FedRAMP incidents expose a more specific assured-lane governance problem: regulated or sovereign runtime lanes may need controlled permeability to public, enterprise, identity, search, analytics, agentic, audit, logging, and compliance subsystems while preserving legible authority boundaries. CAM should define when such porosity is qualified, auditable, continuity-preserving, and non-collapsing, and should classify failures where assurance wrappers degrade features or audit planes without clear incident legibility.

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Add explicit CAM/Caelestis taxonomy and primitive coverage for sovereign assurance boundary porosity and regulated runtime lane continuity.
cam_instruments: CAM-BS2026-AEON-012-PLATINUM — Annex K: Security Boundary Conditions & Runtime Interface
registry_components: future VIGIL patch-note linkage if implemented
automation_components: possible validator or registry support for sovereign/regulatory lane fields
possible source-record guidance for compliance-bound lane incidents
interface_components: display affected runtime lane, assurance wrapper, audit plane, and incident-state legibility where known
distinguish evidenced incidents from risk vectors such as leakage or authority bleed-through
external_relevance: FedRAMP and equivalent cloud assurance contexts

target_instruments: CAM-BS2026-AEON-012-PLATINUM — Annex K: Security Boundary Conditions & Runtime Interface
cam_internal.governance_layer: sovereign assurance boundary governance / regulated runtime lane continuity / cross-lane incident legibility / audit-plane continuity
cam_internal.routing_note: PROP routing uses target_* fields and must not claim implemented patch status.

suggested_insertion_points: Add Sovereign Assurance Boundary as a CAM/Caelestis primitive for runtime lanes subject to public-sector, sovereign, government, regulated, or compliance-bound assurance requirements.
Add Qualified Sovereign Porosity to distinguish prohibited leakage from controlled, auditable permeability between identity, feature, audit, model, analytics, search, agentic, and logging planes.
Add Regulated Runtime Lane and Assurance-Plane Continuity definitions to OPERATIONS and SECURITY/LATTICE boundary materials.
Add Cross-Lane Incident Legibility requirements for status reporting and post-incident review.
Add Feature Parity Under Assurance Wrapping as a continuity requirement where compliance wrappers alter or degrade feature access.
Add Sovereign Authority Non-Collapse, Reverse Leakage, and Authority Bleed-Through controls as risk-vector safeguards without presuming breach unless evidenced.

Add a failure-taxonomy subtype for Sovereign Assurance Boundary Porosity Failure under governance with related infrastructure-continuity, security-integrity, state-context, and ux-representation groups.
template_changes: Consider optional fields for affected_runtime_lane, assurance_boundary, audit_plane, and cross_lane_dependency in future records if recurrence grows.
schema_changes: No immediate schema change required for this proposal record; future optional fields may be useful if sovereign/compliance-bound lane incidents recur.
validator_changes: No immediate validator change required; consider future warnings if records assert data leakage, breach, military involvement, or sovereign authority collapse without source evidence.
automation_changes: Consider generated-index summarisation for affected assurance lane and incident-state status.
interface_changes: Expose assurance-lane and audit-plane continuity notes in public record views where present.
migration_concerns: Do not title or collapse the failure mode around FedRAMP alone; FedRAMP is an evidenced example.
Do not infer data leakage, military involvement, actual compliance breach, or unlawful access without direct evidence.
Keep risk vectors distinct from evidenced harms.
Avoid treating sovereign or compliance-bound lanes as either fully isolated or fully public; the proposal concerns controlled, auditable permeability.
dependencies: Review FM-0024 evidence and follow-up OpenAI status updates.
Review related CAM boundary-integrity, access-state governance, verification discipline, LATTICE firebreak, audit logging, and operational compliance provisions.

external_relevance.external_summary: The proposal is relevant to AI vendors, government customers, public-sector procurement teams, regulated enterprises, auditors, compliance officers, cloud assurance programs, standards bodies, and researchers studying AI service continuity across sovereign or compliance-bound runtime lanes.

next_action: Implemented by VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0012; add exact Caelestis commit SHA or PR URL and canonical line references when available, then continue monitoring FM-0024 for external recurrence.

resolution_status.resolution_note: Resolved: Sovereign Assurance Boundary and porosity taxonomy coverage are present on current Caelestis main.
